items.title: Google DeepMind on X — Gemma 4 in AI Studio
items.author: x.com
items.snippet: Google AI and Google DeepMind have been highly active on X (Twitter) in April 2026, with several major announcements. The most prominent is the release of Gemma 4, a new family of open models available in Google AI Studio, HuggingFace, Kaggle, and Ollama, designed to enable multi-step planning and autonomous agentic workflows on-device. Alongside this, Google DeepMind announced a new autonomous agents capability with native tool use and up to 256K context, enabling AI to plan, navigate apps, and

items.title: Defining AI workflows (Flows) | Genkit - Firebase
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Genkit is an open-source AI framework built and maintained by Google/Firebase, available for JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python. It provides a unified, code-centric interface for building production-ready AI features including chatbots, automations, recommendation systems, and agentic workflows. Core primitives include 'flows' (typed, observable AI functions), tool calling, structured outputs, and multimodal content support. The framework integrates with multiple model providers (Google Gemin

items.title: Top Firebase Studio updates from Google I/O 2025
items.author: firebase.blog
items.snippet: Firebase Studio is Google's agentic, cloud-based development environment that merges Project IDX with Gemini-powered AI agents to help developers build and ship full-stack apps — including games — entirely from the browser. It features an App Prototyping agent ("Prototyper") that allows natural-language, no-code prototyping of web apps, and a Code OSS-based IDE for more traditional development. For game development specifically, Firebase Studio can generate browser-based games (e.g., a 15-puzzle

items.title: Service update: mitigating abuse and prioritizing traffic · google-gemini/gemini-cli Discussion #22970
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: The google-gemini GitHub organization (github.com/google-gemini) remains highly active in early 2026, with the flagship `gemini-cli` repository seeing frequent releases. The CLI — an open-source AI agent that brings Gemini directly into the terminal — follows a weekly release cadence: preview builds drop every Tuesday at 23:59 UTC, and stable promotions follow the next week. As of April 2026, the project is in the v0.37–v0.39 range, with nightly builds dated April 9–10, 2026. Pull requests are s

items.title: Gemini-3-flash-preview GA dates? - Google AI Developers Forum
items.author: discuss.ai.google.dev
items.snippet: The Gemini API has seen significant updates in early 2026. Key launches include the Interactions API (Beta) providing a unified interface for Gemini models and agents, the Gemini Deep Research Agent (preview) for autonomous multi-step research tasks, and enhanced text-to-speech models — Gemini 2.5 Flash TTS and Gemini 2.5 Pro TTS previews — with improved expressivity and pacing. A File Search API was released to public preview for grounding responses in custom data, and gemini-3.1-flash-live-pre

items.title: Google Antigravity: The Agentic IDE Changing Development Work
items.author: www.index.dev
items.snippet: Google Antigravity is Google's new agentic development platform, launched in early 2026, that goes beyond a traditional AI-powered IDE to support multi-agent, task-oriented workflows. It features a dual-interface design: an Editor View (a familiar AI-powered IDE with tab completions and inline commands) and a Manager Surface (a dedicated interface for spawning, orchestrating, and observing multiple agents working asynchronously across editor, terminal, and browser surfaces simultaneously). Agent
